w.description: Width component of the aspect ratio

h.description: Height component of the aspect ratio

aspectRatio.description: The aspect ratio information for the video

isShared.description: Indicates if the video is shared with others

thumbnail.description: URL of the video thumbnail image

animatedThumbnailUrl.description: URL of the animated thumbnail for the video

isTrashed.description: Indicates if the video is in trash

spaceId.description: The ID of the space the video belongs to

spaceName.description: The name of the space the video belongs to

folderId.description: The ID of the folder the video is stored in

creatorUserId.description: User ID of the video creator

ownerId.description: User ID of the video owner

lastModifierId.description: User ID of the person who last modified the video

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the video was last updated

manualSave.description: Last manual save timestamp of the video

autoSave.description: Last auto save timestamp of the video

revision.description: The current revision number of the video

duration.description: The duration of the video in seconds

durationFrames.description: The duration of the video in frames

numScenes.description: The number of scenes in the video

type.description: Content type to retrieve, only "video" is currently supported.

paginationToken.description: If provided, this token retrieves the next set of contents matching the current query. When used, the `order` parameter will be ignored.

limit.description: Specifies the number of results to return per request. If omitted, the default is 50 items per response.

order.description: Determines the order in which results are returned. This parameter is ignored if `paginationToken` is provided.

videos.description: A list of videos returned for the user. This field is included only when the requested content type is "video".

nextPaginationToken.description: A token that can be used to fetch the next page of results.

webhookUrl.description: Callback URL generated by Zapier. Must starts with `https://hooks.zapier.com/`.

person.description: Index of the character to be replaced.

target.description: Target language of the generated video.

style.description: Style of the text-to-speech voice. Available options depends on the voice provider.

speed.description: Speed of the text-to-speech voice. Available options depends on the voice provider.

pitch.description: Pitch of the text-to-speech voice. Available options depends on the voice provider.

text.description: Context or details about the video to be generated.

method.description: Method to use the text or uploaded file for video generation.

extractParams.description: Flag indicating whether video generation settings should be extracted from input data. If true, only `text` or `file` is required.

callbackInfo.description: Callback settings for notifying Zapier the video generation result.

characters.description: Characters to be used in the video. If is not provided for a specific person in the video, default character will be used for that person.

voices.description: Text-to-speech setting for each person in the video. Number of required settings is depend on the layout of choice.

file.description: Uploaded file. Only supported in `multipart/form-data` request.

engine.description:
Engine from voice provider.
Wellsaid voices support `wellsaid-engine` only.
Azure voices support `neural` only.
AWS voices support either one or both of `neural` and `standard`, depending on each voice.
If value not provided, would determine based on the voice.
Note: if multiple engines are supported by the voice, the default value would be the first one of below order: `wellsaid-engine`, `neural`, `standard`

pitch.description: Pitch of the voice. Please note that not all voices support the option.

rate.description: Speed of the voice. Please note that not all voices support the option.

style.description: Tone of the voice. Please note that not all voices support the option. First tone available for the voice would be used as default.

AvatarVoiceParams.description: The voice actor for the AI avatar video. If not provided, an actor based on avatar's gender speaking the provided language would be used.

script.description: The text for the avatar to read.

background.description: Avatar background. By default use the background in avatar from provider.

customBackground.description: UGC asset ID of avatar custom background, which would override `background` parameter.

url.description: URL of the webhook, must be in https

events.description: Events that the webhook subscribed to

secret.description: Secret of the webhook which is required to [verify signatures](#tag/Webhook/Verifying-Vyond-Signatures) from Vyond,             note that this secret is only available at time of webhook creation

url.description: URL of the webhook, must be in https

events.description: Events that the webhook subscribed to

url.description: Webhook URL. Must be in https protocol.

secret.description: Webhook secret for [signing the webhook event request by Vyond](#tag/Webhook/Verifying-Vyond-Signatures). If not provided, there is no signature in request. The secret should be in length 64 and contains only digits and English alphabets.

url.description: URL of the webhook, protocol must be https

events.description: Events that the webhook subscribed to

url.description: URL of the webhook, protocol must be https

events.description: Events that the webhook subscribed to

url.description: Vyond web URL of the video

filter.description: Filter to narrow down the list of items.

schemas.description: The URIs of the SCIM schemas.

totalResults.description: Total number of items matching the query.

userName.description: The user's username. It must be an email address, except for special circumstances.

name.description: The components of the user's name.

active.description: User's administrative status. Setting it to false deactivates the user.

emails.description: The components of email. Only one email address is supported for each user. The email address must be the same as userName, except for special circumstances.

addresses.description: The components of address. Only one address is supported for each user.

tags.name: Introduction
tags.description:
The Vyond API is only available to user with API token or OAuth 2.0 access token.

## Basic

There are 2 sets of APIs:

-   SCIM 2.0 APIs: `https://api.vyond.com/scim/v2/`
-   REST APIs: `https://api.vyond.com/rest/`

REST APIs are versioned `https://api.vyond.com/rest/{version}/{path}`

## Authentication

The Vyond SCIM 2.0 API uses **API tokens** to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API tokens in **Security > SCIM Provisioning**, token expiry and renewal is available on the page if your account has SCIM feature provisioned.

The Vyond REST API uses **OAuth 2.0 access tokens** to authenticate requests. The access token is short-lived and therefore require regular renewal using the refresh token.

Note: Vyond will soon support **API tokens** in REST API authentication.
